The M90/A90 Trunk Road (Gairneybridge to Milnathort) (Temporary 50 mph and 30 mph Speed Restrictions) Order 2013
The Debt Arrangement Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: The Debt Arrangement Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2011 (“the DAS Regulations”) provide a scheme for the repayment of debts in Scotland. They provide for procedure and forms in respect of a repayment arrangement under the scheme, which on approval is described as a debt payment programme (“a programme”).

items.title: The Glasgow City Council Shawfield Dalmarnock Smartbridge Public Road Scheme 2013 Confirmation Instrument 2013
The Registration of Social Workers and Social Service Workers in Care Services (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend and consolidate the Regulation of Care (Fitness of Employees in Relation to Care Services) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2009 (“the 2009 Regulations”). These Regulations impose requirements on care service providers and Social Care and Social Work Improvement Scotland (“SCSWIS”) as regards the social workers and social service workers they employ.

items.title: The Football Banning Orders (Regulated Football Matches) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order, which comes into force on the day after the day on which it is made, modifies section 55(3) of the Police, Public Order and Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2006.

items.title: The Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2013 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Order 2010 (“the 2010 Order”) which was amended by the Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2012 and the Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2013. The 2010 Order designates sports grounds, classes of sporting events played at those grounds and classes of sporting events outside Great Britain for the purposes of Part II of the Criminal Law (Consolidation) (Scotland) Act 1995 (sporting events: control of alcohol etc.).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Messengers-at-Arms and Sheriff Officers Rules) (Amendment) 2013
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Act of Sederunt (Messengers-at-Arms and Sheriff Officers Rules) 1991 in respect of several matters.

items.title: The Highland Council Rum Harbour Revision (Transfer) Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends the constitution of Rum harbour by transferring it from the control of Scottish Natural Heritage to that of the Highland Council.

items.title: The A738 Trunk Road (Kilwinning) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting and Use of Road) Order 2013
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 7) Order 2013
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 7) Order 2013
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 7) Order 2013
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 7) Order 2013
Act of Sederunt (Registration Appeal Court) 2013 (revoked)
items.description: This Act of Sederunt appoints three judges to hear registration appeals under section57 of the Representation of the People Act 1983. It revokes the Act of Sederunt (Registration Appeal Court) 2012, which previously made such provision.

items.title: The A7 Trunk Road (High Street and Townhead, Langholm) (Temporary Prohibition On Use of Road) Order 2013
Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 5) (Miscellaneous) 2013
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Rules of the Court of Session.

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Amendment (No. 3) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Regulations 2013 to correct an error in a reference to earlier Regulations.

items.title: The Public Services Reform (Planning) (Local Review Procedure) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997. Section43A(8)(c) enables an applicant to require the planning authority to review the case if the person appointed to determine the application has not done so within the period prescribed by regulations made under that section. The amendment made by article2 will allow the applicant and the appointed person to extend that period by agreement.

items.title: The A85 Trunk Road (Perth Road/East High Street/High Street, Crieff) (Highland Gathering) (Temporary Prohibition On Use of Road) Order 2013
Act of Sederunt (Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules Amendment) (Policing and Crime Act 2009) 2013
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Summary Application Rules in consequence of the amendments made to the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 by the Policing and Crime Act 2009. In particular, it inserts new rule3.19.20A, which provides for applications to the sheriff under section127N of the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (Discharge, variation and lapse of detention order) to be made by minute in the process of the application to extend the detention order. It also inserts rule 3.19.20B, which specifies the procedure for appealing to the Court of Session under sections127O (appeals against further detention or discharge or variation of detention order) and 131C (appeals in relation to property seized under section131A).

items.title: The A85 Trunk Road (Perth Road/East High Street/High Street, Crieff) (Pipe Band Championship) (Temporary Prohibition On Use of Road) Order 2013
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 8) Order 2013
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 8) Order 2013
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 8) Order 2013
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 8) Order 2013
The Civil Contingencies Act 2004 (Contingency Planning) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations provide for Strategic Co-ordinating Groups to be re-named Regional Resilience Partnerships, and the agglomeration of the co-ordination areas from eight to three, by amendment of the Civil Contingencies Act 2004 (Contingency Planning) (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”).

items.title: The M898/A898 Trunk Road (Erskine Bridge) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic and 40mph Speed Restriction) (No. 2) Order 2013
The Aquaculture and Fisheries (Scotland) Act 2013 (Commencement and Transitional Provisions) Order 2013
items.description: This Order appoints 16th September 2013 as the day on which the remaining provisions of the Aquaculture and Fisheries (Scotland) Act 2013 (“the Act”) come into force. Sections 4 (meaning of “wellboat”), 22 (interpretation of Part 1, Chapter 3) and 53 (interpretation of Part 3) and Part 6 (general) came into force on the day after Royal Assent, which was granted on 18th June 2013.

items.title: The Public Services Reform (Planning) (Pre-application consultation) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends section35A of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997 to provide that applications made under section42 of that Act are not subject to the requirements for pre-application consultation contained in section35B of that Act.

items.title: The Legal Aid and Advice and Assistance (Photocopying Fees and Welfare Reform) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend four sets of Regulations concerning advice and assistance and legal aid.

items.title: The A92 Trunk Road (Leuchars) (Temporary 30mph Speed Restriction) Order 2013
The Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 (Commencement No. 9) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force section 23 of the Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”) on 21st September 2013. Section 23 of the Act empowers the Scottish Ministers to make regulations containing provision for or in connection with the fixing of charges for the provision of water services for the purposes of the protection of the water environment.

items.title: The Home Energy Assistance Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Home Energy Assistance Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2013 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for the making of grants to improve the thermal insulation and energy efficiency of dwellings and to provide advice to reduce or prevent the wastage of energy in a dwelling.

items.title: The Marine Navigation Act 2013 (Commencement) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings sections 1 to 6 of the Marine Navigation Act 2013 (“the Act”) into force in Scotland on 1st October 2013. These sections concern pilotage matters, harbour directions and harbour closure orders.

items.title: The A85 Trunk Road (Crieff) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2013
The Fish Labelling (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations enforce, in Scotland, the consumer information requirements of Chapter 2 of Title 1 of Council Regulation (EC) No 104/2000 on the common organisation of the markets in fishery and aquaculture products and Commission Regulation (EC) No 2065/2001 laying down detailed rules for the application of Council Regulation (EC) No 104/2000 as regards informing consumers about fishery and aquaculture products.

items.title: The A85 Trunk Road (Comrie) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2013
The Glasgow Commonwealth Games (Enforcement Officers) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision as to whom the Organising Committee of the Glasgow Commonwealth Games may designate as an enforcement officer. Section 21 of the Glasgow Commonwealth Games Act 2008 already allows the designation of an inspector of weights and measures as an enforcement officer. These Regulations permit a person to be designated as an enforcement officer if that person is authorised to enforce the provisions of section 92 of the Trade Marks Act 1994 and the council which granted the authorisation approves the designation.

items.title: The Glasgow Commonwealth Games (Games Locations) Order 2013
items.description: In terms of section 1(2) of the Glasgow Commonwealth Games Act 2008 (“the 2008 Act”), a “Games location” is a place in which an event is held as part of the Games and any other place specified by order which is used in connection with the Games. This Order specifies the Merchant City and George Square, Glasgow as Games locations. Cultural events are to be held in these places in connection with the Games.

items.title: The Planning etc. (Scotland) Act 2006 (Supplementary and Consequential Provisions) Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends the provisions of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997 (“the Act”). The changes are minor supplementary and consequential provisions following from the amendments made to the Act by the Planning etc. (Scotland) Act 2006 (“the 2006 Act”).

items.title: The Glasgow Commonwealth Games Act 2008 (Commencement No. 4) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st October 2013 all the provisions of the Glasgow Commonwealth Games Act 2008 (“the Act”) which are not yet in force. Those provisions are—

items.title: The Personal Licence (Training) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: The Licensing (Scotland) Act 2005 makes provision for regulating the sale of alcohol. Section 76 of that Act provides for the issuing of personal licences and other provisions in the Act regulate the circumstances in which a personal licence is required. Section 87 of that Act provides that a personal licence holder must comply with such requirements as to training as may be prescribed.

items.title: The Scottish Civil Justice Council and Criminal Legal Assistance Act 2013 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force various sections in Part 2 of the Scottish Civil Justice Council and Criminal Legal Assistance Act 2013 (“the 2013 Act”) on 11th October 2013.

items.title: The A7 Trunk Road (Lindean Mill Junction to the B6360 Abbotsford House Junction) (Temporary 30mph Speed Restriction) Order 2013
The Overhead Lines (Exemption) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations replace and revoke the Overhead Lines (Exemption) Regulations 1990 (“the 1990 Regulations”) in relation to their application to Scotland.

items.title: The Common Agricultural Policy Single Farm Payment and Support Schemes (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations insert a new regulation 6A into the Common Agricultural Policy Single Farm Payment and Support Schemes (Scotland) Regulations 2011 (S.S.I. 2011/416) to implement the provisions of Article 10b of Council Regulation (EC) No 73/2009 (OJ L 30, 31.1.2009, p.16) and Commission Implementing Decision 2013/146/EU (OJ L 82, 22.3.2013, p.58) for 2013.

items.title: The Food Additives, Flavourings, Enzymes and Extraction Solvents (Scotland) Regulations 2013
The Angus College (Transfer and Closure) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: On 1st November 2013 Angus College will cease to exist.

items.title: The Banff and Buchan College of Further Education (Transfer and Closure) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: On 1st November 2013 Banff and Buchan College of Further Education will cease to exist.

items.title: The Cumbernauld College (Transfer and Closure) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: On 1st November 2013 Cumbernauld College will cease to exist.

items.title: The A9 Trunk Road (Kessock Bridge) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns and Use of Specified Lanes) Order 2013
The John Wheatley College and Stow College (Transfer and Closure) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: On 1st November 2013 John Wheatley College and Stow College will both cease to exist.

items.title: The Scottish Civil Justice Council and Criminal Legal Assistance Act 2013 (Commencement No. 2) Amendment Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends the Scottish Civil Justice Council and Criminal Legal Assistance Act 2013 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2013 so that section 20 of the Scottish Civil Justice Council and Criminal Legal Assistance Act 2013, which concerns contributions for criminal legal aid, does not come into force on 11th October 2013.

items.title: The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 9) Order 2013
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 9) Order 2013
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 9) Order 2013
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 9) Order 2013
The Marine (Scotland) Act 2010 (Commencement No. 3 and Consequential Provisions) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force those provisions of the Marine (Scotland) Act 2010 (“the Act”) which have yet to come into force and with the coming into force of this Order the Act is fully commenced.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Marine Fish Farming) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations replace and revoke the Town and Country Planning (Marine Fish Farming) (Scotland) Regulations 2007. They set out how an application for planning permission for the operation of a marine fish farm is to be made to the Scottish Ministers under section 31A of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997. Regulations 2 and 4 make provision for the information to be provided by an applicant and regulation 3 sets out when certain bodies must be consulted on an application. Regulation 5 also revokes the Town and Country Planning (Marine Fish Farming) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2011 and the Town and Country Planning (Marine Fish Farming) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2012 which are spent.

items.title: The Freedom of Information (Scotland) Act 2002 (Designation of Persons as Scottish Public Authorities) Order 2013
items.description: This Order makes provision for the designation of persons as a Scottish public authority under section 5 of the Freedom of Information (Scotland) Act 2002 (“the 2002 Act”).

items.title: The Bee Keeping (Colonsay and Oronsay) Order 2013
items.description: This Order prohibits the keeping of animals of the genus Apis (honey bees) on the adjoining islands of Colonsay and Oronsay other than animals of the subspecies Apis mellifera mellifera (a “black bee”).

items.title: The A9 and A82 Trunk Roads (Kessock Bridge) (Temporary Prohibitions on Use and Speed Limits) Order 2013
The Loch Sligachan, Isle of Skye, Scallops Several Fishery Order 2013
items.description: This Order confers on David Neil Oakes for a period of 15 years the right of several fishery for scallops in part of Loch Sligachan (article 2). The part of Loch Sligachan to which the Order applies is described in the Schedule. The location of the area is also shown on the map which is described in article 1(2). A representation of the map, for illustrative purposes only, is appended to this Note.

items.title: The Post-16 Education (Scotland) Act 2013 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force the following provisions of the Post-16 Education (Scotland) Act 2013 (“the Act”) on 10th October 2013: sections 1; 6(2); 13; 21 and paragraphs 2(7)(d), 3 and 8(5)(a)(i) and (22)(a) to (c) of the schedule to the Act.

items.title: The Public Contracts (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Public Contracts (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The A82 Trunk Road (Dumbarton) (40mph Speed Limit) Variation Order 2013
The A92/A972 Trunk Road (Balfarg Junction) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2013
The A6091/A7 Trunk Road (Buccleuch Road and Buccleuch Street, Hawick) (Temporary Prohibition on Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2013
The Marine Licensing (Pre-application Consultation) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations provide for certain classes or descriptions of licensable marine activity to be subject to a requirement for pre-application consultation to be undertaken in terms of sections 22 to 24 of the Marine (Scotland) Act 2010 (“the 2010 Act”).

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Amendment (No. 4) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the principal CTR Regulations”) and the Council Tax Reduction (State Pension Credit) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the SPC CTR Regulations”).

items.title: The Rosyth International Container Terminal (Harbour Revision) Order 2013
items.description: This Order, made following an application by Port Babcock Rosyth Limited (“the Company”), empowers the Company to construct, maintain and operate in the Forth and on land at Rosyth in Fife, a container terminal at the port and port related facilities known as Port Babcock Rosyth.

items.title: The Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Conservation Bodies) Amendment Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003 (Conservation Bodies) Order 2003 by adding Perth and Kinross Heritage Trust to the list of prescribed conservation bodies. Conservation bodies are bodies in favour of whom it is competent to create a conservation burden under section 38(1) of the Title Conditions (Scotland) Act 2003.

items.title: The Water Environment (Drinking Water Protected Areas) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order identifies those bodies of water used for the abstraction of drinking water, as required by section 6(1) of the Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the Act”). In doing so, it implements the Scottish Ministers’ obligation to identify such bodies of water as set out in paragraph 1 of Article 7 of Directive 2000/60/EC of the European Parliament and of the Council establishing a framework for Community action in the field of water policy (OJ L 327, 22.12.2000, p.1).

items.title: The Glasgow Commonwealth Games (Trading and Advertising) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: The Glasgow Commonwealth Games Act 2008 makes it an offence to trade (other than in a building) in the vicinity of a Games location at a prohibited time (“the trading offence”). It also makes it an offence to advertise in the vicinity of a Games location at a prohibited time (“the advertising offence”). A Games location is a place in which an event is held as part of the Games and any other place used in connection with the Games which is specified by order.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Commissary Business) 2013
items.description: This Act of Sederunt consolidates and restates the Act of Sederunt (Commissary Business) 1975 (“the 1975 Act of Sederunt”) with modifications.

items.title: The National Health Service (Cross-Border Health Care) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations which extend to Scotland, implement the requirements for national contact points and the reimbursement of charges set out in the Directive 2011/24/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council on the application of patients' rights in cross-border healthcare (“the Directive”).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Summary Applications, Statutory Applications and Appeals etc. Rules Amendment) (Miscellaneous) 2013
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes amendments to the Summary Application Rules in respect of proceedings under the Adults with Incapacity (Scotland) Act 2000 and under the Proceeds of Crime Act 2002 (External Investigations) Order 2013.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 6) (Miscellaneous) 2013
items.description: Paragraph 2 inserts new rules into Chapter38 (Reclaiming) and Chapter40 (Appeals from inferior courts) in respect of the urgent disposal of reclaiming motions and appeals. In cases where the reclaimer or appellant appeals against an interlocutor containing an order under section11(1) of the Children (Scotland) Act 1995 (court orders relating to parental responsibilities etc.), those new rules provide that the reclaimer or appellant must enrol for urgent disposal of the reclaiming motion or appeal.

items.title: The A96 Trunk Road (Inveramsay Bridge Improvement) (Trunking and Detrunking) Order 2013
The A96 Trunk Road (Inveramsay Bridge Improvement) (Side Roads) Order 2013
The A9 Trunk Road (Ballinluig) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turns) Order 2013
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2013
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2013
The Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (Scotland) Order 2013 (revoked)
items.description: This Order restates with amendments the Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (Scotland) Order 2012 (“the 2012 Order”).

items.title: The A82 Trunk Road (Aberchalder Bridge) (Temporary Prohibition of Traffic) Order 2013
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2013
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 10) Order 2013
The Drugs Courts (Scotland) Amendment Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends the Drugs Courts (Scotland) Order 2003 to remove the reference to the Sheriffdom of Tayside, Central and Fife from the Schedule. By virtue of this amendment, the sheriffdom is no longer required to have a drugs court in accordance with section 42 of the Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2003.

items.title: The Growth and Infrastructure Act 2013 (Commencement) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings section 21(4) to (6) of the Growth and Infrastructure Act 2013 into force on 1st December 2013.

items.title: The Electricity Generating Stations (Applications for Variation of Consent) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision in Scotland about the procedures for handling applications to vary consents for the construction, extension and operation of electricity generating stations that have been granted under section 36 of the Electricity Act 1989 (“section 36 consents”).

items.title: The Fruit Juices and Fruit Nectars (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations implement Council Directive 2001/112/EC relating to fruit juices and certain similar products intended for human consumption, as last amended by Directive 2012/12/EU. They revoke and replace the Fruit Juices and Fruit Nectars (Scotland) Regulations 2003 (as amended).

items.title: The A96 Trunk Road (Church Road, Keith) (Temporary Prohibition On Use of Road) Order 2013
The Animal By-Products (Enforcement) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations revoke, in relation to Scotland, the Animal By-Products (Identification) Regulations 1995 and revoke and remake the Animal By-Products (Enforcement) (Scotland) Regulations 2011, incorporating certain provisions of the Animal By-Products (Identification) Regulations 1995.

items.title: The Whitehills Harbour and Marina (Constitution) Order 2013
items.description: This Order re-constitutes the Whitehills Harbour Commissioners in line with the recommendations of Modern Trust Ports for Scotland – Guidance for Good Governance published by Transport Scotland in November 2012.

items.title: The Rural Development Contracts (Land Managers Options) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Rural Development Contracts (Land Managers Options) (Scotland) Regulations 2008 (“the 2008 Regulations”).

items.title: The Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981 (Variation of Schedules A1 and 1A) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: Section 22(1)(a) of the Wildlife and Countryside Act 1981 (“the 1981 Act”) provides for the variation of Schedules A1 and 1A to that Act by order.

items.title: The Adoption (Recognition of Overseas Adoptions) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations come into force on 3rd January 2014. They revoke and replace the Adoption (Designation of Overseas Adoptions) Order 1973 (“the 1973 Order”). The effect of these Regulations is to specify the countries and territories whose adoption orders are classed as ‘overseas adoptions’ in terms of section 67 of the Adoption and Children (Scotland) Act 2007 (“the 2007 Act”).

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (East Dunbartonshire Council) Designation Order 2013
items.description: This Order extends to East Dunbartonshire Council, arrangements for enforcing parking controls already available in London and certain other areas in England and in Edinburgh, Glasgow, Perth and Kinross, Aberdeen, Dundee, South Lanarkshire, Renfrewshire, East Ayrshire, South Ayrshire, East Renfrewshire and Fife.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Parking Adjudicators) (East Dunbartonshire Council) Regulations 2013 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the procedure to be followed in relation to appeals before parking adjudicators against decisions of the parking authority under a decriminalised parking regime in East Dunbartonshire local government area (regulations 3-16). The parking adjudicators are appointed under section 73 of the Road Traffic Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”). That section, along with other provisions of that Act relating to the decriminalisation of certain parking offences, is applied with modifications, where appropriate, to East Dunbartonshire Council parking area by the Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (East Dunbartonshire Council) Designation Order 2013.

items.title: The Parking Attendants (Wearing of Uniforms) (East Dunbartonshire Council Parking Area) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations prescribe functions during the exercise of which, a parking attendant must wear such uniform as the Scottish Ministers may determine (regulation 2).

items.title: The Flood Risk Management (Designated Responsible Authorities) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order designates as ‘responsible authorities’ for the purposes of the Flood Risk Management (Scotland) Act 2009 (“the Act”), the following public bodies and office-holders—

items.title: The Litter (Fixed Penalties) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: Sections 33A and 88 of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 provide for the payment of a fixed penalty to discharge any liability to conviction for the waste (including littering and flytipping) and littering offences under sections 33 and 87 respectively of that Act.

items.title: The M90/A90 Trunk Road (Great Western Road) (Temporary Prohibition of Specified Turn) Order 2013
Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment No. 7) (Miscellaneous) 2013
items.description: This Act of Sederunt amends the Rules of the Court of Session. Paragraph2 amends rule24.6(4) (applications to amend the name of a party in more than one cause). Paragraph3 amends rule76.37(3) (civil recovery investigations: disclosure orders). Paragraph4 amends rule104.5(1) (Justice and Security Act 2013: hearing on applications).

items.title: The Protected Trust Deeds (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision about how voluntary trust deeds entered into by debtors for the benefit of their creditors become protected from action by creditors, the consequences of a trust deed being granted that status, the rights of creditors, the discharge of the debtor and trustee from the trust deed, and the administration of trust deeds.

items.title: The Fundable Bodies (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: The Further and Higher Education (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the Act”) established the Scottish Further and Higher Education Funding Council and made provision relating to the funding of bodies which provide further and higher education. Those bodies are described in the Act as “fundable bodies” and are listed in schedule 2 to the Act.

items.title: The Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Criminal Legal Aid (Scotland) (Fees) Regulations 1989 (“the principal Regulations”) as regards the fees payable to solicitors for carrying out legal aid work in relation to solemn criminal proceedings.

items.title: The Pollution Prevention and Control (Designation of Energy Efficiency Directive) (Scotland) Order 2013 (revoked)
items.description: This Order designates Directive 2012/27/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council on energy efficiency (the “Energy Efficiency Directive”) as a relevant directive for the purposes of paragraph 20 of Schedule 1 to the Pollution Prevention and Control Act 1999 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Long Leases (Scotland) Act 2012 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force the Long Leases (Scotland) Act 2012 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Water Environment (River Basin Management Planning: Further Provision) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make further provision for the protection of the water environment.

items.title: The Water Environment (Shellfish Water Protected Areas: Designation) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order designates 84 areas of coastal water or transitional water within the Scottish River Basin District as “shellfish water protected areas” for the purposes of Part 1 (protection of the water environment) of the Water Environment and Water Services (Scotland) Act 2003.

items.title: The Water Environment (Shellfish Water Protected Areas: Environmental Objectives etc.) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make further provision in relation to shellfish water protected areas within the Scotland River Basin District in order to support shellfish life and growth and to contribute to the high quality of shellfish products suitable for human consumption.

items.title: The Seed (Scotland) (Miscellaneous Amendments) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations implement Commission Implementing Directive 2013/45/EU (OJ L 213, 8.8.2013, p.20). They amend the Vegetable Seeds Regulations 1993 in order to change the botanical name of tomato in Schedule 1 to those Regulations.

items.title: The National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) (No. 2) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make amendments to the National Health Service (Travelling Expenses and Remission of Charges) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2003 (“the 2003 Regulations”).

items.title: The Budget (Scotland) Act 2013 Amendment Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends the Budget (Scotland) Act 2013 (“the Act”).

items.title: The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2013
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2013
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2013
The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 11) Order 2013
The Food Safety, Food Hygiene and Official Controls (Sprouting Seeds) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the General Food Regulations 2004 (regulation 2), the Food Hygiene (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (regulation 3) and the Official Feed and Food Controls (Scotland) Regulations 2009 (regulation 4) in order to provide for the enforcement of the following EU Regulations:—

items.title: The Health Boards (Membership) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations revoke and replace the Health Boards (Membership) (Scotland) Regulations 2009 (“the 2009 Regulations”).

items.title: The Adoption (Recognition of Overseas Adoptions) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations correct the defect in the Adoption (Recognition of Overseas Adoptions) (Scotland) Regulations 2013 (“the 2013 Regulations”) and come into force on 3rd January 2014.

items.title: The Food (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations implement Commission Regulation (EU) No 1079/2013 laying down transitional measures for the application of Regulations (EC) No 853/2004 and (EC) No 854/2004 of the European Parliament and of the Council (“the Commission Regulation”). They also implement Commission Implementing Regulation (EU) No 702/2013 on transitional measures for the application of Regulation (EC) No 882/2004 of the European Parliament and of the Council as regards the accreditation of official laboratories carrying out official testing for Trichinella (“the Commission Implementing Regulation”). In addition they correct a minor error in the Food Safety Act 1990 (“the 1990 Act”).

items.title: The Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends the Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (Scotland) Order 2013 (“the 2013 Order”).

items.title: The A77 Trunk Road (Dalrymple Street Girvan) (Temporary Prohibition On Use of Road) Order 2013
The Defamation Act 2013 (Commencement) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force sections 6 and 7(9) of the Defamation Act 2013 (“the 2013 Act”) in so far as those sections extend to Scotland. Sections 6, 7(9), 15, 16(5) (in so far as it relates to sections 6 and 7(9)) and 17 of the 2013 Act extend to Scotland.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Levying) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the Non-Domestic Rates (Levying) (Scotland) (No. 3) Regulations 2012 to correct an error in regulation 6 of that instrument, which revokes the Non-Domestic Rates (Levying) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (S.S.I.2012/28).

items.title: The Feed (Hygiene and Enforcement) and Animal Feed (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Feed (Hygiene and Enforcement) (Scotland) Regulations 2005 (“the 2005 Regulations”) (regulation 2) and the Animal Feed (Scotland) Regulations 2010 (“the 2010 Regulations”) (regulation 3) in order to provide for the enforcement of Commission Regulation (EU) No 225/2012 amending Annex II to Regulation (EC) No 183/2005 of the European Parliament and of the Council as regards the approval of establishments placing on the market, for feed use, products derived from vegetable oils and blended fats and as regards the specific requirements for production, storage, transport and dioxin testing of oils, fats and products derived thereof (“Regulation 225/2012”).

items.title: The Sexual Offences (Scotland) Act 2009 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force on 16th December 2013 sections 52 and 53(2)(a) to (d) of the Sexual Offences (Scotland) Act 2009 (“the 2009 Act”). Section 52 abolishes the common law offences of rape, clandestine injury to women, lewd, indecent or libidinous practice or behaviour and sodomy. Section 53(2)(a) to (d) makes transitional provision related to the abolition of these offences.

items.title: The Water Resources (Scotland) Act 2013 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force, on 21st December 2013, the following provisions of the Water Resources (Scotland) Act 2013 (“the Act”)—

items.title: The A9 Trunk Road (Blair Atholl Junction) (Temporary Prohibition on Use of Road) Order 2013
The A96 Trunk Road (Inverurie Road/Auchmill Road/Great Northern Road) (Redetermination of Means of Exercise of Public Right of Passage) Order 2013
Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) 2013
items.description: This Act of Sederunt substitutes a new Table of Fees for the Table of Fees in Schedule 1 to the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Sheriff Officers) (No.2) 2002, by substituting a new Table of Fees.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) 2013
items.description: This Act of Sederunt substitutes a new Table of Fees for the Table of Fees in Schedule1 to the Act of Sederunt (Fees of Messengers-at-Arms) (No.2) 2002.

items.title: The National Health Service (Variation of Areas of Health Boards) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order varies the areas of Scotland for which Health Boards are constituted to the extent necessary to align the boundaries of Health Board areas and local authority areas.

items.title: The Post-16 Education (Scotland) Act 2013 (Commencement No. 2) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force the following provisions of the Post-16 Education (Scotland) Act 2013 (“the Act”) on 13th January 2014: sections 5(1); 18; 20; 21 and paragraphs 2(1) and (2)(b) and 8(1) and (23)(a)(i), (iii) and (iv) of the schedule to the Act.

items.title: The Environmental Protection (Restriction on Use of Lead Shot) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Environmental Protection (Restriction on Use of Lead Shot) (Scotland) (No. 2) Regulations 2004 (which prohibit the use of lead shot for the purpose of shooting with a shot gun on or over wetlands) to create an exception in respect of the clay target event at the Commonwealth Games 2014.

items.title: The Police Service of Scotland Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of constables of the Police Service of Scotland.

items.title: The Town and Country Planning (Prescribed Date) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations specify the date prescribed for the purposes of section 26AA(2) of the Town and Country Planning (Scotland) Act 1997 (“the Act”). These Regulations replace the Town and Country Planning (Prescribed Date) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 which are revoked by regulation 3. The prescribed date remains 31st March 2014 for all cases other than for those marine fish farms when an application for planning permission has been made to the Scottish Ministers before the date on which these Regulations come into force. In such cases the prescribed date is 31st March 2015.

items.title: The Local Governance (Scotland) Act 2004 (Remuneration) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Local Governance (Scotland) Act 2004 (Remuneration) Regulations 2007. They substitute increased amounts for different categories of local authority remuneration to their members, for the year to 31st March 2014 and from 1st April 2014.

items.title: The A702 Trunk Road (Biggar High Street) (Temporary Prohibition On Use of Road) Order 2013
The A85 Trunk Road (Comrie) (Temporary Prohibition On Use of Road) Order 2013
The Colleges of Further Education (Transfer and Closure) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: On 22nd February 2014 the following colleges (“the closing colleges”) will cease to exist—

items.title: The National Health Service (General Ophthalmic Services) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (General Ophthalmic Services) (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the Regulations”), which provide for arrangements for the provision of general ophthalmic services under the National Health Service in Scotland, and for the preparation and maintenance by each Health Board of an Ophthalmic List.

items.title: The Land Reform (Scotland) Act 2003 (Modification) Order 2013
items.description: This Order modifies section 7(1) of the Land Reform (Scotland) Act 2003 (“the 2003 Act”).

items.title: The Register of Young Voters (Anonymous Entries) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order makes provision in connection with the anonymous registration of young voters for the purposes of an independence referendum.

items.title: The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2013
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2013
The Non-Domestic Rating (Valuation of Utilities) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends the Non-Domestic Rating (Valuation of Utilities) (Scotland) Order 2005 (“the 2005 Order”) and comes into force on 1stApril 2013.

items.title: The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2013
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 12) Order 2013
The Scottish Charitable Incorporated Organisations (Removal from Register and Dissolution) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Scottish Charitable Incorporated Organisations (Removal from Register and Dissolution) Regulations 2011 (“the 2011 Regulations”). Regulation 8 of the 2011 Regulations currently makes provision about the steps that must be taken in relation to removal from the Scottish Charity Register and dissolution of any Scottish Charitable Incorporated Organisation (“SCIO”) which no longer meets the charity test under section 7 of the Charities and Trustee Investment (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the 2005 Act”), including where a SCIO has failed to comply with a direction from the Office of the Scottish Charity Regulator (“OSCR”) as regards meeting the charity test.

items.title: The Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (Scotland) Amendment (No. 3) Order 2013
items.description: This Order correct errors in the Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (Scotland) Amendment (No. 2) Order 2013 and amends the Bovine Viral Diarrhoea (Scotland) Order 2013 (“the principal Order”).

items.title: The Health Boards (Membership and Elections) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order revokes the Health Boards (Membership and Elections) (Scotland) Act 2009 (Commencement No. 1) Order 2009, which commenced sections 1 to 3 of the Health Boards (Membership and Elections) (Scotland) Act 2009 in respect of Fife and Dumfries and Galloway Health Board areas for the purpose of piloting Health Board elections in those areas. This Order brings the pilot scheme to an end in terms of section 6 of the 2009 Act. The Order also makes incidental, consequential and saving provision.

items.title: The Freedom of Information (Scotland) Act 2002 (Historical Periods) Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends section 57(1) of the Freedom of Information (Scotland) 2002 Act (“the Act”) to make alterations to the definition of when a record containing information becomes a historical record for the purposes of that Act.

items.title: The Plant Health (Scotland) Amendment (No. 3) Order 2013 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Plant Health (Scotland) Order 2005 (S.I. 2005/613).

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rating (Unoccupied Property) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Non-Domestic Rating (Unoccupied Property) (Scotland) Regulations 1994 (“the principal Regulations”) as regards premises that, under those Regulations, attract a reduced non-domestic rate liability.

items.title: The Tobacco and Primary Medical Services (Scotland) Act 2010 (Commencement No. 3) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force sections1, 2 and 3 of the Tobacco and Primary Medical Services (Scotland) Act 2010 (“the 2010 Act”) (article2). Sections1, 2 and 3 are brought into force for the purpose of making regulations on 26thFebruary 2013, for large shops on 29th April 2013 and for all other purposes on 6th April 2015.

items.title: The Police Service of Scotland (Promotion) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to the eligibility and procedure for the promotion of constables of the Police Service of Scotland.

items.title: The Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2013 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Order 2010 (“the 2010 Order”) which was amended by the Sports Grounds and Sporting Events (Designation) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2012. The 2010 Order designates sports grounds, classes of sporting events played at those grounds and classes of sporting events outside Great Britain for the purposes of Part II of the Criminal Law (Consolidation) (Scotland) Act 1995 (sporting events: control of alcohol etc.).

items.title: The National Assistance (Sums for Personal Requirements) (Scotland) Regulations 2013 (revoked)
items.description: Section 22(4) of the National Assistance Act 1948 as applied by section 87(3) and (4) of the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 (“the 1968 Act”) requires a local authority to assume in assessing a person’s liability to pay for accommodation provided under the 1968 Act or section 25 of the Mental Health (Care and Treatment) (Scotland) Act 2003 that they will need for their personal requirements such sum per week as may be prescribed by regulations.

items.title: The National Assistance (Assessment of Resources) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2013 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Assistance (Assessment of Resources) Regulations 1992 (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations concern the assessment of a person’s liability to pay for accommodation provided under the Social Work (Scotland) Act 1968 (“the 1968 Act”). By virtue of section 87(3) of the 1968 Act, accommodation provided under the 1968 Act or section 25 of the Mental Health (Care and Treatment) (Scotland) Act 2003 shall be regarded as accommodation provided under Part III of the National Assistance Act 1948.

items.title: The Police Service of Scotland (Police Cadets) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of police cadets of the Police Service of Scotland.

items.title: The Police Service of Scotland (Special Constables) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision in respect of special constables of the Police Service of Scotland.

items.title: The Local Government Finance (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order—

items.title: The Council Tax (Variation for Unoccupied Dwellings) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision for council tax variations for dwellings which have no resident.

items.title: The Caledonian Maritime Assets (Lochaline Ferry Services Slipway) Harbour Empowerment Order 2013
items.description: This Order, made following an application by Caledonian Maritime Assets Limited (“CMAL”), empowers CMAL to maintain and operate at Lochaline in the Highland Region the harbour and harbour related facilities.

items.title: The Police and Fire Reform (Scotland) Act 2012 (Commencement No. 3 and Transitory Provision) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force on 15th February 2013 paragraph 33(15) of schedule 7 to the Police and Fire Reform (Scotland) Act 2013 which amends section 103(4)(a) of the Police, Public Order and Criminal Justice (Scotland) Act 2006 (“the 2006 Act”).

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Reduction (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Council Tax Reduction (State Pension Credit) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Council Tax Reduction (State Pension Credit) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the principal Regulations”).

items.title: The Plant Health (Scotland) Amendment Order 2013 (revoked)
items.description: This Order amends the Plant Health (Scotland) Order 2005 (“the principal Order”) and includes measures to prevent the introduction and spread of certain plant pests, in particular,Clavibacter michiganensisssp.sepedonicus(Spieckerman and Kotthoff) Daviset al., a cause of potato ring rot andEpitrix cucumeris(Harris),Epitrix similaris(Gentner),Epitrix subcrinita(Lec.) andEpitrix tuberis(Gentner), known commonly as potato flea beetles.

items.title: The Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exclusions and Exceptions) (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order consolidates the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (Exclusions and Exceptions) (Scotland) Order 2003 (“2003 Order”) which excludes and makes exceptions to the provisions of section 4 (effect of rehabilitation) of the Rehabilitation of Offenders Act 1974 (“the Act”). It excludes further proceedings and types of work from certain provisions within that section and updates the terms used to reflect recent legislation, including the Legal Services (Scotland) Act 2010 and the Electronic Money Regulations 2011.

items.title: The Police and Fire Reform (Scotland) Act 2012 (Commencement No. 4, Transitory and Transitional Provisions) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force on 1st April 2013 the provisions of the Police and Fire Reform (Scotland) Act 2012 (“the Act”), to the extent that those provisions are not already in force.

items.title: The National Health Service (Scotland) (Injury Benefits) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Scotland) (Injury Benefits) Regulations 1998 (“the 1998 Regulations”) as follows:

items.title: The Personal Injuries (NHS Charges) (Amounts) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Personal Injuries (NHS Charges) (Amounts) (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the principal Regulations”) which make provision in relation to the amount of NHS charges which a person (who pays compensation to an injured person) is liable to pay, where that injured person has received National Health Service treatment or ambulance services.

items.title: The North East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2013
The North West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No.2) Order 2013
The South East Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2013
The South West Scotland Trunk Roads (Temporary Prohibitions of Traffic and Overtaking and Temporary Speed Restrictions) (No. 2) Order 2013
The Electricity (Applications for Consent) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Electricity (Applications for Consent) Regulations 1990 (“the principal Regulations”). The principal Regulations apply in relation to applications for consent under section 36 of the Electricity Act 1989 (“the 1989 Act”) to construct, extend, operate or change the manner of operation of a generating station, or under section 37 of the 1989 Act to install or keep installed an electric line above ground.

items.title: The Fees in the Registers of Scotland (Consequential Provisions) Amendment Order 2013 (revoked)
items.description: This Order makes a consequential amendment to the Fees in the Registers of Scotland Order 1995 by removing the fee payable to the Keeper of the Registers of Scotland fixed in relation to services not listed elsewhere in the Order. The consultancy, advisory and commercial services offered by the Keeper will continue to be provided for, and fees charged as agreed with those provided with the services, under section 108 of the Land Registration etc. (Scotland) 2012.

items.title: The Restriction of Liberty Order etc. (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations principally regulate matters concerned with the provisions in the Criminal Procedure (Scotland) Act 1995 under which an offender’s movements may be restricted, and under which compliance with such restrictions may be monitored. The Regulations also regulate matters concerned with the provisions in the Children (Scotland) Act 1995 under which a children’s hearing can impose a movement restriction condition on a child.

items.title: The Police Service of Scotland (Conduct) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the procedures for dealing with cases where an act or omission of a constable of the Police Service of Scotland (“the Police Service”) below the rank of assistant chief constable is alleged to amount to misconduct.

items.title: The Police Service of Scotland (Performance) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the procedures for dealing with cases where the performance of a constable of the Police Service of Scotland (“the Police Service”) is unsatisfactory.

items.title: The Police Service of Scotland (Senior Officers) (Conduct) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision for the procedures for dealing with cases where an act or omission of a senior officer of the Police Service of Scotland (“the Police Service”) is alleged to amount to misconduct.

items.title: The Police Appeals Tribunals (Scotland) Rules 2013
items.description: These Rules make provision as to the procedure on appeals by constables under section 56 of the Police and Fire Reform (Scotland) Act 2012 (“the 2012 Act”) to the Police Appeals Tribunal (“the Tribunal”) established by that Act.

items.title: The Education (School Lunches) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (School Lunches) (Scotland) Regulations 2009 so as to prescribe universal credit (payable under Part 1 of the Welfare Reform Act 2012) for the purposes of paragraphs (a)(iv) and (b)(iii) of section 53(3) of the Education (Scotland) Act 1980. The effect is that where a pupil is, or the pupil’s parents are, in receipt of universal credit and the pupil is receiving school lunches, the education authority must not charge for the lunches.

items.title: The Welfare Reform (Consequential Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make amendments of secondary legislation in consequence of the introduction of the allowance known as personal independence payment under Part 4 of the Welfare Reform Act 2012. The provisions of most significance are as follows.

items.title: The A977 Trunk Road (Feregait and Toll Road, Kincardine) (Temporary Prohibition of Waiting, Loading and Unloading) Order 2013
The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (East Renfrewshire Council) Designation Order 2013
items.description: This Order extends to East Renfrewshire Council, arrangements for enforcing parking controls already available in London and certain other areas in England and in Edinburgh, Glasgow, Perth and Kinross, Aberdeen, Dundee, South Lanarkshire, Renfrewshire, East Ayrshire and South Ayrshire. It does so by designating East Renfrewshire local government area (with a specified exception) as a permitted parking area and as a special parking area in accordance with Schedule3 to the Road Traffic Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”). The Order applies, to the designated area, various provisions of the 1991 Act and modifies them where necessary. It also makes consequential modifications to certain provisions of the Road Traffic Regulation Act 1984 (“the 1984 Act”) dealing with parking and related matters.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Parking Adjudicators) (East Renfrewshire Council) Regulations 2013 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the procedure to be followed in relation to appeals before parking adjudicators against decisions of the parking authority under a decriminalised parking regime in East Renfrewshire local government area (regulations3-16). The parking adjudicators are appointed under section 73 of the Road Traffic Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”). That section, along with other provisions of that Act relating to the decriminalisation of certain parking offences, is applied with modifications, where appropriate, to the parking area established by the Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (East Renfrewshire Council) Designation Order 2013.

items.title: The Parking Attendants (Wearing of Uniforms) (East Renfrewshire Council Parking Area) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations prescribe functions during the exercise of which, a parking attendant must wear such uniform as the Scottish Ministers may determine (regulation2).

items.title: The Housing (Scotland) Act 2001 (Assistance to Registered Social Landlords and Other Persons) (Grants) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Housing (Scotland) Act 2001 (Assistance to Registered Social Landlords and Other Persons) (Grants) Regulations 2004 (“the 2004 Regulations”). The 2004 Regulations provide the mechanics for several grant schemes that are operated by local authorities.

items.title: The National Health Service (Superannuation Scheme and Pension Scheme) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service Superannuation Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2011 and the National Health Service Pension Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2008 so as to provide for changes to pensionable pay bands or earnings bands and employee contribution percentage rates for the 2013-14 Scheme Year (which is the period from 1st April 2013 to 31st March 2014).

items.title: The Teachers’ Superannuation (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Teachers’ Superannuation (Scotland) Regulations 2005 to adjust the tiering structure and the rates of contributions payable by teachers during 2013/14 in respect of their pensions.

items.title: Act of Adjournal (Criminal Procedure Rules Amendment) (Miscellaneous) 2013
items.description: This Act of Adjournal amends the Criminal Procedure Rules 1996.

items.title: The Scottish Police Authority (Provision of Goods and Services) Order 2013
items.description: This Order specifies the types of goods and services that the Scottish Police Authority and the Police Service of Scotland (as arranged by the Scottish Police Authority) can provide and the types of persons to whom they can provide these goods and services.

items.title: The Financial Assistance for Environmental Purposes (Scotland) Order 2013
items.description: This Order varies section 153(1) of the Environmental Protection Act 1990 (c.43) (“the 1990 Act”) so as to—

items.title: The Individual Learning Account (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Individual Learning Account (Scotland) Regulations 2011 (“the 2011 Regulations”). The amendments have the effect of changing requirements for Qualifying Persons within the meaning of the 2011 Regulations so that those participating in certain training programmes will no longer be eligible for grant. These Regulations also make certain savings and transitional provision for those currently in receipt of grant (regulation 3).

items.title: The Police Service of Scotland (Temporary Service) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to constables of the Police Service of Scotland who are engaged on temporary service outwith the Police Service.

items.title: The Late Payment of Commercial Debts (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations implement Directive 2011/7/EU of the European Parliament and of the Council of 16th February 2011 on combating late payment in commercial transactions.

items.title: The Non-Domestic Rates (Enterprise Areas) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Non-Domestic Rates (Enterprise Areas) (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) and come into force on 1st April 2013.

items.title: The Public Transport Users’ Committee for Scotland (Removal of Functions) Order 2013
items.description: This Order removes from the Public Transport Users’ Committee for Scotland all the functions which it presently has. The functions concerned are those under subsection (1) of section 42 of the Transport (Scotland) Act 2005 (considering and making recommendations to Scottish Ministers about public transport services), under subsection (2) of that section (considering and making recommendations about any matter if asked to do so by the Scottish Ministers) and under article 4 of the Public Transport Users’ Committee for Scotland Order 2006 (determining certain complaints concerning the operation of bus services).

items.title: The Scottish Road Works Register (Prescribed Fees) Regulations 2013 (revoked)
items.description: Section 112A(1) of the New Roads and Street Works Act 1991 (“the Act”) (as inserted by section19 of the Transport (Scotland) Act 2005) provides for the Scottish Road Works Commissioner (“the Commissioner”) to keep a register to be known as the Scottish Road Works Register (“the SRWR”).

items.title: The Education (Fees, Awards and Student Support) (Miscellaneous Amendments) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Education (Access Funds) Regulations 1990 (“the Access Funds Regulations”), the Repayment of Student Loans (Scotland) Regulations 2000 (“the Repayment Regulations”), the Education (Student Loans for Tuition Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2006 (“the SLTF Regulations”), the Education Authority Bursaries (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the Bursaries Regulations”), the Nursing and Midwifery Student Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the Nursing Regulations”), the Education (Fees and Awards) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the 2007 Fees Regulations”), the Students’ Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the Allowances Regulations”), the Education (Student Loans) (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the Loans Regulations”), the Education Maintenance Allowances (Scotland) Regulations 2007 (“the EMA Regulations”) and the Education (Fees) (Scotland) Regulations 2011 (“the 2011 Fees Regulations”).

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Rules of the Court of Session Amendment) (Protective Expenses Orders in Environmental Appeals and Judicial Reviews) 2013
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes amendments to the Rules of the Court of Session 1994.

items.title: The Private Rented Housing (Scotland) Act 2011 (Commencement No. 6 and Savings Provisions) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings sections3, 5, 8, 9, 11 and 12 of the Private Rented Housing (Scotland) Act 2011 (“the 2011 Act”) into force on 1stApril 2013 and section6 of the 2011 Act into force on 1stJune 2013.

items.title: The Food (Miscellaneous Amendment and Revocation) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend regulation 20 of the Materials and Articles in Contact with Food (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (“the 2012 Regulations”) to make its meaning clearer.

items.title: The Food Safety (Sampling and Qualifications) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations, in Scotland, revoke (at regulation 12 and Schedule 4) and remake with amendments provisions of the Food Safety (Sampling and Qualifications) Regulations 1990 (S.I. 1990/2463).

items.title: The Sale of Tobacco (Display of Tobacco Products and Prices etc.) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations make provision in relation to the display of tobacco or smoking related products in specialist tobacconists (regulation 3) and in other retail premises (regulation 4). They also make provision in relation to the display of prices of tobacco products and smoking related products (regulations 8 to 17).

items.title: The Police Federation (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations provide for a change in the Scottish Police Federation’s constitution and proceedings.

items.title: The Council Tax (Information-sharing in relation to Council Tax Reduction) (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations provide for circumstances in which a Revenue and Customs official may supply information which is held by Her Majesty’s Revenue and Customs for council tax purposes in Scotland.

items.title: The A9 Trunk Road (Golspie) (Prohibition of Waiting) Order 2013
The Police Pensions (Contributions) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Police Pensions Regulations 1987 (as regards Scotland) and the Police Pensions (Scotland) Regulations 2007 to adjust the pension contributions of police officers as from 1st April 2013. These Regulations also amend both the 1987 and 2007 Regulations to correct errors contained in the Police Pensions (Contributions) Amendment (Scotland) Regulations 2012 (S.S.I. 2012/71).

items.title: The Less Favoured Area Support Scheme (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Less Favoured Area Support Scheme (Scotland) Regulations 2010 (“the 2010 Regulations”). The 2010 Regulations make provision for the purposes of the implementation of Council Regulation (EC) No 1698/2005 on support for rural development by the European Agricultural Fund for Rural Development (EAFRD) (OJ L 277, 21.10.2005, p.1), Commission Regulation (EC) No 1974/2006 (OJ L 368, 23.12.2006, p.15) and Commission Regulation (EU) No 65/2011 (OJ L 25, 28.1.2011, p.8). The latter two Regulations lay down detailed rules for the application of Council Regulation 1698/2005.

items.title: The Tenant Information Packs (Assured Tenancies) (Scotland) Amendment Order 2013
items.description: This Order amends the Schedule contained in the Tenant Information Packs (Assured Tenancies) (Scotland) Order 2013. The Schedule provides a Tenant Information Pack that persons who are to be landlords under assured tenancies are to provide to persons who are to be their tenants.

items.title: Act of Sederunt (Sheriff Court Rules) (Lay Representation) 2013
items.description: This Act of Sederunt makes amendments to the Ordinary Cause Rules, the Summary Application Rules, the Summary Cause Rules and the Small Claim Rules.

items.title: The Criminal Legal Aid (Fixed Payments) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the Criminal Legal Aid (Fixed Payments) (Scotland) Regulations 1999 S.I.1999/491 (“the 1999 Regulations”) to provide that in certain circumstances, and subject to certain conditions, the Scottish Legal Aid Board (“the Board”) may determine that a solicitor is not to receive fixed payments for work done in connection with a grant of assistance by way of representation but instead is to receive payment based on the amount of time spent and work done in providing the assistance by way of representation.

items.title: The Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (Fife Council) Designation Order 2013
items.description: This Order extends to Fife Council, arrangements for enforcing parking controls already available in London and certain other areas in England and in Edinburgh, Glasgow, Perth and Kinross, Aberdeen, Dundee, South Lanarkshire, Renfrewshire, East Ayrshire and South Ayrshire. A similar Order has been laid before the Scottish Parliament in relation to East Renfrewshire Council, and is due to come into force on 2nd April 2013.

items.title: The Parking Attendants (Wearing of Uniforms) (Fife Council Parking Area) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations prescribe functions during the exercise of which, a parking attendant must wear such uniform as the Scottish Ministers may determine (regulation2).

items.title: The Road Traffic (Parking Adjudicators) (Fife Council) Regulations 2013 (revoked)
items.description: These Regulations prescribe the procedure to be followed in relation to appeals before parking adjudicators against decisions of the parking authority under a decriminalised parking regime in Fife local government area (regulations3-16). The parking adjudicators are appointed under section 73 of the Road Traffic Act 1991 (“the 1991 Act”). That section, along with other provisions of that Act relating to the decriminalisation of certain parking offences, is applied with modifications, where appropriate, to the parking area established by the Road Traffic (Permitted Parking Area and Special Parking Area) (Fife Council) Designation Order 2013.

items.title: The National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Amendment Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations amend the National Health Service (Optical Charges and Payments) (Scotland) Regulations 1998 (“the principal Regulations”) which provide for payments to be made by means of a voucher system, in respect of costs incurred by certain categories of persons in connection with the supply, replacement and repair of optical appliances.

items.title: The Scottish Fire and Rescue Service (Framework and Appointed Day for Strategic Plan) Order 2013 (revoked)
items.description: This Order gives effect to the framework document entitled “Fire and Rescue Framework for Scotland 2013”, for the purpose of section 40(4) of the Fire (Scotland) Act 2005 (“the Act”) (article 2).

items.title: The Children’s Hearings (Scotland) Act 2011 (Commencement No. 7) Order 2013
items.description: This Order brings into force on 31st March 2013 certain provisions of the Children’s Hearings (Scotland) Act 2011 (“the Act”).

items.title: The Children’s Hearings (Scotland) Act 2011 (Transfer of Children to Scotland – Effect of Orders made in England and Wales or Northern Ireland) Regulations 2013
items.description: These Regulations apply where a child is transferred to Scotland from England and Wales or Northern Ireland whilst subject to an order made in that jurisdiction giving an authority care or supervisory responsibility for that child.

items.title: The Church of England Marriage(Amendment) Measure 2012 (Appointed Day) Instrument 2013
items.description: This Instrument brings into force, on 1st June 2013, section 1 of the  Church of England Marriage (Amendment) Measure 2012.

items.title: The Clergy Discipline (Amendment) Measure 2013 (Appointed Day No. 1) Instrument 2013
items.description: This Instrument brings into force, on 1st July 2013, provisions in the Clergy Discipline (Amendment) Measure 2013 in respect of the issuing of a Code of Practice by the Clergy Discipline Commission pursuant to section 39 of the Clergy Discipline Measure 2003 .

items.title: The Diocese in Europe Measure 2013 (Appointed Day) Instrument 2013
items.description: This Instrument brings into force the provisions of the Diocese in Europe Measure 2013. In accordance with the power conferred by the Measure, it makes different provision for different sections.

items.title: Canterbury City Council Act 2013
items.description: An Act to confer powers on Canterbury City Council for the better control of street trading in the city of Canterbury.

items.title: Leeds City Council Act 2013
items.description: An Act to confer powers on Leeds City Council for the better control of street trading in the city of Leeds.

items.title: Nottingham City Council Act 2013
items.description: An Act to to confer powers on Nottingham City Council for the better control of street trading in the city of Nottingham.

items.title: Reading Borough Council Act 2013
items.description: An Act to confer powers on Reading Borough Council for the better control of street trading and touting in the borough of Reading.

items.title: London Local Authorities and Transport for London Act 2013
items.description: An Act to confer further powers upon local authorities in London and upon Transport for London; and for related purposes.

items.title: Humber Bridge Act 2013
items.description: An Act to amend the constitution of the Humber Bridge Board and to confer new borrowing and other powers on it; to make new provision for the recovery of any deficit of the Board from local authorities in the area; to confer new powers for the setting and revision of tolls and to make other provision for and in connection with the operation of the bridge; and for connected purposes.

items.title: City of London (Various Powers) Act 2013
items.description: An Act to amend the provision for the control of street trading in the City of London; to make provision relating to City walkways; and for related purposes.

items.title: The Aln Estuary Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Isles of Scilly Marine Conservation Zones Designation Order 2013
The Kingmere Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Lundy Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Manacles Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Medway Estuary Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The North East of Farnes Deep Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Padstow Bay and Surrounds Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Pagham Harbour Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Poole Rocks Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Skerries Bank and Surrounds Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Beachy Head West Marine Conservation Zones Designation Order 2013
The South Dorset Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The South-West Deeps (West) Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Swallow Sand Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Tamar Estuary Marine Conservation Zones Designation Order 2013
The Thanet Coast Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Torbay Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Upper Fowey and Pont Pill Marine Conservation Zones Designation Order 2013
The Whitsand and Looe Bay Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Blackwater, Crouch, Roach and Colne Estuaries Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Canyons Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Chesil Beach and Stennis Ledges Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Cumbria Coast Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The East of Haig Fras Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Folkestone Pomerania Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
The Fylde Marine Conservation Zone Designation Order 2013
Trusts (Capital and Income) Act 2013
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to capital and income in trusts.

items.title: Scrap Metal Dealers Act 2013
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to scrap metal dealers; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Prisons (Property) Act 2013
items.description: An Act to make provision for the destruction of certain property found in prisons and similar institutions.

items.title: Supply and Appropriation (Anticipation and Adjustments) Act 2013
items.description: An Act to authorise the use of resources for the years ending with 31 March 2010, 31 March 2011, 31 March 2012, 31 March 2013 and 31 March 2014; to authorise the issue of sums out of the Consolidated Fund for the years ending with 31 March 2013 and 31 March 2014; and to appropriate the supply authorised by this Act for the years ending with 31 March 2010, 31 March 2011, 31 March 2012 and 31 March 2013.

items.title: Presumption of Death Act 2013
items.description: An Act to make provision in relation to the presumed death of missing persons; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Mobile Homes Act 2013
items.description: An Act to amend the law relating to mobile homes.

items.title: Antarctic Act 2013
items.description: An Act to make provision consequential on Annex VI to the Protocol on Environmental Protection to the Antarctic Treaty; to amend the Antarctic Act 1994; and for connected purposes.

items.title: Welfare Benefits Up-rating Act 2013
items.description: An Act to make provision relating to the up-rating of certain social security benefits and tax credits.

items.title: Jobseekers (Back to Work Schemes) Act 2013
items.description: An Act to make provision about the effect of certain provisions relating to participation in a scheme designed to assist persons to obtain employment and about notices relating to participation in such a scheme.

items.title: Justice and Security Act 2013
